retrieval_text: Sumach is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 115 works and 243 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Sumach, Sumach Berries. Common plant-part co-mentions include Berry, Bark, Root, Leaf, Seed, Gum. Common preparation co-mentions include Tea, Powder, Decoction, Oil, Poultice, Infusion. Common condition co-mentions include Wounds or ulcers, Dysentery, Fever, Rheumatism, Cold or catarrh, Cough. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Toxicity watch, Child-use caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Pregnancy caution, Abortifacient warning. Representative source works include The American vegetable practice : or a new and improved guide to health, designed for the use of families : in six parts; The American vegetable practice, or a new and improved guide to health, designed for the use of families : in six parts / by Morris Mattson.; The American vegetable practice, or a new and improved guide to health, designed for the use of families ... / By Morris Mattson.; An improved system of botanic medicine: founded upon correct physiological principles : embracing a concise view of anatomy and physiology : together with an illustration of the new theory of medicine : to which is added, a treatise on female complaints, midwifery, and the diseases of children (Volume 2). This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
